sources.title: McKinsey Quantum Technology Monitor 2026: A Commercial Tipping Point
claims_supported: $12.6B invested in quantum computing in 2025 — a 6.3-fold increase from prior year
Quantum industry revenue exceeded $1B globally for first time in 2025
33% of analyzed enterprises allocate >$10M annually to quantum initiatives
$2.7 trillion in potential economic value over the next decade
1 qualified quantum candidate per 3 open roles (talent crisis)

sources.title: Meet Willow, our state-of-the-art quantum chip
claims_supported: Willow (105-qubit chip) completed a benchmark in under 5 minutes vs 10^25 years for classical supercomputer
First practical demonstration of exponential quantum error suppression as qubits scale
Error rates decrease as system scales — reversed from prior quantum systems

sources.title: Department of Commerce Announces Letters of Intent for $2 Billion to Accelerate US Leadership in Quantum Computing
claims_supported: US DoC announced $2.013B in CHIPS Act incentives for 9 quantum companies (May 2026)
IBM expected to receive approximately $1B of total allocation
National Quantum Initiative reauthorized with $1.8B in additional funding (2025-2029)

sources.title: Quantum Computing Market Size, Share & Trends — Report 2025-2030
claims_supported: Quantum computing market projected from $3.52B (2025) to $20.2B (2030) at 41.8% CAGR
North America holds largest regional share

sources.title: Quantum Computing Market Size, Value | Growth Analysis 2026-2034
claims_supported: Quantum computing market projected from $2.04B (2026) to $18.33B (2034) at 31.60% CAGR

sources.title: Big Tech's Quantum Computing Investments: Google, IBM, and Microsoft by the Numbers
claims_supported: Global quantum investment reached $17.3B in 2026
Total public commitments from 30+ countries exceed $40B
China leads public investment at ~$15B; EU second at ~$10B; US third at ~$5B

sources.title: IBM Study Finds Quantum Computing Is Coming, But Enterprises Aren't Ready
claims_supported: 59% of executives believe quantum-enabled AI will transform their industry by 2030
Only 27% expect their organizations to be using quantum computing

sources.title: Quantum Computing Applications: 8 Real-World Use Cases in 2026
claims_supported: Logistics firms report 15-30% improvements in route planning from quantum-classical hybrid deployments
Pharma R&D using quantum simulation for molecular modeling and binding affinity prediction
Financial services piloting quantum for portfolio optimization and derivatives pricing

sources.title: IQM State of Quantum Report: Industry Must Solve Talent Shortage and Software Platforms
claims_supported: Talent shortage is a critical industry barrier alongside software platform immaturity
Workforce shortage extends beyond hardware to algorithm and application development skills

sources.title: Quantum Computing Statistics 2026: Market Size and Data
claims_supported: Global quantum market reached $1.9B in 2025 (broader market definition)
More than 50% of quantum companies project 11%+ revenue growth from 2025 to 2026
37% project revenue increases exceeding 25%

sources.title: US to Award $2 Billion to Quantum Companies, Take Equity Stakes
claims_supported: US government taking equity stakes in quantum companies as part of CHIPS Act quantum investment
IBM expected to receive ~$1B; GlobalFoundries ~$375M

sources.title: Google Debuts New Quantum Chip, Error Correction Breakthrough, and Roadmap Details
claims_supported: Willow chip error rates 20x better than predecessor Sycamore
Google's quantum roadmap details steps toward fault-tolerant quantum computation

unverified_claims.note: Estimated based on historical Semrush/Ahrefs ranges for this term; not directly sourced from keyword tool in this research session

unverified_claims.claim: Market share percentages for IBM (~30%), Google (~20%), Microsoft (~15%), IonQ (~12%), D-Wave (~8%)
unverified_claims.note: Multiple sources confirm market is fragmented; top 10 players represent ~40-50% of market (MarketsandMarkets). Individual share estimates are approximations from analyst commentary, not formally published share data — marked [est] in trend-data.json
2021 baseline (~$0.5B) extrapolated backward from MarketsandMarkets 41.8% CAGR; not directly quoted from a single source covering both endpoints
